entries.reason: Use frontier planners plus cheaper workers to preserve quality while slashing multi-agent coding cost.
Planner-worker coordination post independently supports the role split and explains why the hierarchy beats flat coordination.
Treat resource configuration as a first-class variable in coding-agent evaluations.
Build tool evaluations before prompt polish, and optimize MCP tools around real tasks.
Broader agent-building guidance reinforces evaluator-optimizer loops and careful tool-interface design.
Break long cloud-agent jobs into short durable workflows with decoupled state.
Environment-as-code and environment governance details corroborate the cloud-agent durability and verification story.
Use tiered reasoning and selective escalation so coding-agent cost follows task difficulty.
Useful-intelligence-per-dollar framing supports measuring model routing by cost per successful task instead of token price.
